MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON JUL-13-2017 IN 206, PLAINTIFFS ADRIENNE AND DANIEL MCNALLY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. MICHAEL MAZZOCONE APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFFS. JOHN LOFTON APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT DENIES WITHOUT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE. THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED ALL THE FACTS PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1332(D) INCLUDING THE EXTREME PROXIMITY OF THE TRIAL DATE, PREVIOUS 2 CONTINUANCES OF THE TRIAL DATE, LENGTH OF THE CONTINUANCE AND THE PREJUDICE TO PARTIES AS WELL AS THE FACTS PRODUCED BY THE DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION TO CONTINUE. THERE ARE CURRENTLY NO DISCOVERY MOTIONS PENDING AND THE LAST DAY TO HEAR DISCOVERY MOTION HAS EXPIRED UNDER CRC AND CCP. THE JUDGE PRO TEM HAS ISSUED AN ORDER ON 6/28/17 STATING THAT "ALL DOCUMENTS REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFFS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED OR MADE AVAILABLE TO PLAINTIFFS." IN TERMS OF THE MERITS OF PLAINTIFFS' VERY LATE MOTION TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, IT WILL BE LEFT FOR JUDGE KAHN TO HEAR THE MOTION. THE COURT URGES THE PLAINTIFFS TO SEEK AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME TO ADVANCE THE HEARING DATE ON THE MOTION TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THEREFORE, THE COURT DENIES WITHOUT PREJUDICE AND GIVES JUDGE KHAN THE DISCRETION TO RECONSIDER THE MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL, IN THE EVENT HE WOULD GRANT THE MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Michael Mazzocone telephone number 415-399-0800 for Plaintiffs Adrienne McNally and Daniel McNally.
John Lofton of Leland, Parachini, Steinberg, Matzger & Melnick LLP telphone number 415-957-1800 for Defendants Killing Kevin, Inc., Patrick & Friends and Jill Williamson.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JILL WILLIAMSON, KILLING KEVIN INC. and PATRICK & FRIENDS INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for involuntary dissolution, sixth cause of action for accounting and seventh cause of action for declaratory relief and overruled as to the fourth cause of action for fraud and fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Plaintiffs do not, nor does it appear that they can, allege sufficient grounds for either involuntary dissolution or an accounting. Because plaintiffs seek coercive relief in the form of their contract and tort claims, there is no need for their declaratory relief claim. Plaintiffs have adequately pled claims for fraud and breach of fiduciary duty. Plaintiff's Counsel is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
